meta.title: Unit 1 — Singular & Plural Nouns (Test 1)
meta.subject: English Grammar
meta.description: Identify singular and plural nouns and form plurals with -s and -es.

questions.question: Which word is a singular noun?
questions.explanation: "dog" is a singular noun — it means just one dog.

questions.question: What is the plural of "cat"?
questions.explanation: Add -s to most nouns to make them plural. cat → cats.

questions.question: "Buses" is the correct plural form of "bus".
questions.explanation: Yes! Words ending in -s get -es added: bus → buses.

questions.question: The plural of "box" is "boxs".
questions.explanation: Words ending in -x get -es added: box → boxes, not boxs.

questions.question: There are many ___ in the sky. (star)
questions.explanation: "many" tells us we need a plural. star → stars (add -s).

questions.question: Write the plural form of "watch".
questions.explanation: watch → watches. Words ending in -ch add -es to form the plural.

questions.question: Write the plural form of "tree".
questions.explanation: tree → trees. Regular nouns just add -s.

questions.question: She has three ___. (dish)
questions.explanation: dish → dishes. Words ending in -sh add -es.

questions.question: Sort these words into Singular or Plural.
questions.explanation: cat/bird/kite = one thing (singular). boxes/buses = more than one (plural).

questions.question: Sort these plurals: did they add -s or -es?
questions.explanation: Regular nouns add -s. Nouns ending in -ch, -ss add -es.

questions.question: The plural of "class" is "classs".
questions.explanation: Words ending in -ss add -es: class → classes.

questions.question: Write the plural form of "box".
questions.explanation: box → boxes. Words ending in -x add -es.

questions.question: I see five ___ outside. (car)
questions.explanation: Five tells us we need a plural. car → cars.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: "Three bears are by the tree."

questions.question: In the sentence "Mom buys seven apples," the word "apples" is a plural noun.
questions.explanation: Yes! "apples" is the plural form of "apple" — seven tells us there are many.

questions.question: Write the plural form of "brush".
questions.explanation: brush → brushes. Words ending in -sh add -es.

questions.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: She finds ten shell on the beach.
She finds ten shells on the beach.
She finds ten shelles on the beach.
She finds ten shelves on the beach.
questions.explanation: shell → shells (add -s). "ten" tells us we need a plural.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: "Roy has two glasses of juice."

questions.question: Which word follows the rule: 'ending in -sh → add -es'?
questions.explanation: dish ends in -sh, so it becomes dishes by adding -es.

questions.question: In the sentence "We see many church," the noun is correctly written.
questions.explanation: "church" ends in -ch, so its plural is "churches". We need "many churches".
